Petition For Approval Of Compromise And Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Continued To Nov-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Scorsine for The Temple.
Warren Woo for Xin Lu (Heir/Respondent).
Janet Boessenecker for San Francisco Public Administrator ('PA").

The Court noted that the Public Administrator and Yi Shi are not a party to the settlement, that it appears Mr. Shi has disclaimed his interest, but has not signed anything on that, that the agreement seems to exclude third party claims and suggest that title is being passed free and clear of any claims from anyone, and questions where the Parties stand as to this agreement. Mr. Woo stated that the Public Administrator has an interest in the estate as a stakeholder and has no interest in the assets themselves, that the PA has not really been involved, and that he contacted them and they (PA) were not interested in participating because they are a neutral party. Mr. Woo also stated that as far as the third parties' rights, his understanding is that Mr. Scorsine has gotten signed documents by all of them either by disclaimers or assignments, and that the third parties have always received notice. The Court questions how it knows that all of the parties waive their rights and ask if Mr. Scorsine has received something from all of the creditors/mortgage holders. Mr. Woo stated that if the 850 petition is granted and none of these assets are part of the estate, his Client and The Temple can settle anyway they want, and that if the property is an asset of the estate, his Client will get it from them. Mr. Scorsine stated that he does not have disclaimers from the mortgagees, that they have an 850 petition first to determine the component of the estate, that if granted, then the estate has nothing in it and that he believes any incumbrances were going to be cleared upon turning over the property to Ms. Lu. The Court noted that it does not see that in the settlement agreement. Mr. Scorsine stated that perhaps the way to approach this is that the 850 needs to be approved or not approved. Mr. Boessenecker stated that the concern is that the 850 will result in a trial and that they are trying to avoid a trial. The Court noted that the parties could re-negotiate the settlement now and include any third parties, or re-notice the 850 petition. The Petition for Approval of Compromise and Settlement Agreement is continued to November 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Scorsine is to re-notice the 850 petition for November 5, 2019.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Settling Real And Personal Property In Title To The Shean-Mih-Yuan-Tung Temple Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-23-2013 Continued To Sep-24-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator; Avery Tam for the Shean Mih Yuan Tung Temple, Inc.; John Scorsine also for the Shean Mih Yuan Tung Temple, Inc.; Xin Zhu Lu, Decedent's Niece, appearing in propria persona; Qing Yu Chang, Decedent's Grand-Nephew, appearing in propria persona.

The Court addressed the motion to admit counsel pro hac vice noting that there was a corrected proof of service on file. There being no objection by counsel, the Court then Granted the motion to admit counsel pro hac vice (subject to the written order). Counsel is to prepare the order. The Court presented two issues with regard to the 850 petition: the lack of a verification and lack of personal service of the petition. Mr. Scorsine stated that the verification would be done and that they were taking waivers of service, etc. Mr. Ferdon presented his concerns and requested that the petition for instructions continue to trail. Mr. Scorsine stated that they are working towards regulatory compliance re: the temple. The Court inquired as to who would get the property and if there was someone that could sign a verfication. Mr. Scorsine stated that the property will go to the Temple as an organization and that the Abbess could sign the verification. Mr. Ferdon stated that they want to avoid this in future, that they'd like to know who it is being transferred to before the transfer occurs, and they'd like to know the organizational background. The Court addressed the proposed order noting that the current order contains requests outside the scope, suggested that Mr. Scorsine look at his prayer re: transferring the property, and stated that the order should reflect what's in the prayer. The petition for order settling real and personal property in title to the Shean Mih Yuen Tung Temple and the petition for instructions are continued to September 24, 2013 at 10:30 a.m. in Dept. 204 for receipt of the waivers and/or disclaimers, receipt of a proposed order which should conform to the prayer, and for the information regarding the Temple's corporate structure. The Court stated that once the information was received that it would be reviewed by the Probate Examiner for any procedural issues and then a determination can be made as to whether or not the matter needs to be set for hearing. The Court requested all courtesy copies be submitted to Dept. 204 by September 17, 2013.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
